howItWorks.intro: This boxing reach calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Reach = Wingspan ÷ 2 (arm length from center chest)
Reach Advantage = Your Wingspan - Opponent Wingspan
Positive ape index = longer arms relative to height
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is the ape index?
faq.answer: Ape index is your wingspan minus your height. A positive ape index means your arms are longer than you are tall — an advantage in boxing, climbing, and swimming. The average is 0 (wingspan equals height).

faq.question: How important is reach in boxing?
faq.answer: Reach is one of the most significant physical advantages. Fighters with longer reach can hit opponents while staying out of range. However, shorter-armed fighters can overcome this with head movement and inside fighting.

faq.question: What is the average boxing reach?
faq.answer: Average reach is approximately equal to height. Professional boxers average about 71-73 inches at middleweight. Notable long reaches include Tyson Fury (85") and Thomas Hearns (78").

faq.question: Is the Boxing Reach Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard sports analytics formulas trusted by athletes, coaches, and fans. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Boxing Reach Calculator?
sections.body: The Boxing Reach Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes, coaches, and fans who need quick, accurate calculations in the sports analytics space. By entering your height, wingspan, opponent height, you get instant results including your reach, your ape index, reach advantage. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting your reach right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In sports analytics, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes, coaches, and fans worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to analyze performance with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your height and need to find the right your reach. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Height (inches)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your height — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: wingspan, opponent height, opponent wingspan. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your your reach, your ape index, reach advantage. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your height before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both your reach and your ape index — they work together to give you the full picture.
